members.name: _DrawEditorGizmo
members.summary: Used for drawing [b]editor-only[/b] modification gizmos. This function will only be called in the Godot editor and can be overridden to draw custom gizmos. [b]Note:[/b] You will need to use the Skeleton2D from [method SkeletonModificationStack2D.get_skeleton] and it's draw functions, as the [SkeletonModification2D] resource cannot draw on its own.

members.name: _Execute
members.summary: Executes the given modification. This is where the modification performs whatever function it is designed to do.

members.name: _SetupModification
members.summary: Called when the modification is setup. This is where the modification performs initialization.

members.name: ClampAngle
members.signature: public float ClampAngle(float angle, float min, float max, bool invert)
members.summary: Takes an angle and clamps it so it is within the passed-in [param min] and [param max] range. [param invert] will inversely clamp the angle, clamping it to the range outside of the given bounds.

members.name: GetEditorDrawGizmo
members.summary: Returns whether this modification will call [method _draw_editor_gizmo] in the Godot editor to draw modification-specific gizmos.

members.name: GetIsSetup
members.summary: Returns whether this modification has been successfully setup or not.

members.name: GetModificationStack
members.summary: Returns the [SkeletonModificationStack2D] that this modification is bound to. Through the modification stack, you can access the Skeleton2D the modification is operating on.

members.name: SetEditorDrawGizmo
members.summary: Sets whether this modification will call [method _draw_editor_gizmo] in the Godot editor to draw modification-specific gizmos.

members.name: SetIsSetup
members.summary: Manually allows you to set the setup state of the modification. This function should only rarely be used, as the [SkeletonModificationStack2D] the modification is bound to should handle setting the modification up.

members.name: Enabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the modification's [method _execute] function will be called by the [SkeletonModificationStack2D].

members.name: ExecutionMode
members.summary: The execution mode for the modification. This tells the modification stack when to execute the modification. Some modifications have settings that are only available in certain execution modes.

virtualMethods.name: _DrawEditorGizmo
virtualMethods.summary: Used for drawing [b]editor-only[/b] modification gizmos. This function will only be called in the Godot editor and can be overridden to draw custom gizmos. [b]Note:[/b] You will need to use the Skeleton2D from [method SkeletonModificationStack2D.get_skeleton] and it's draw functions, as the [SkeletonModification2D] resource cannot draw on its own.

virtualMethods.name: _Execute
virtualMethods.summary: Executes the given modification. This is where the modification performs whatever function it is designed to do.

virtualMethods.name: _SetupModification
virtualMethods.summary: Called when the modification is setup. This is where the modification performs initialization.
